name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram is a global money transfer service offering domestic and international money sending/receiving at thousands of agent locations including retailers, banks, and check-cashing stores.
description_long: MoneyGram is an established money transfer company that has built a vast network of physical locations across the United States and internationally. The company operates through a partnership model, embedding its services within existing retail locations rather than operating standalone branches. This strategy allows MoneyGram to leverage the foot traffic and infrastructure of established retailers to provide financial services to consumers. MoneyGram's core offering centers on money transfer capabilities—both sending and receiving funds domestically and internationally. Customers can initiate transfers online or complete transactions at physical agent locations. The company positions itself as offering convenience through accessibility, with 29 locations listed in Cleveland alone across partners like CVS, Walmart, Ace Cash Express, and independent retailers. MoneyGram's distribution network includes major retailers (Walmart, CVS), specialized check-cashing services (Ace Cash Express), and small convenience stores, giving it broad geographic reach across income levels and neighborhoods. What distinguishes MoneyGram is its hybrid online-offline model; customers can start transfers online and pick up funds in-person, or conduct entire transactions at agent locations without requiring a bank account. The integration with major retailers makes the service accessible during extended hours—many locations operate until 8 PM or later. However, MoneyGram operates in a competitive landscape dominated by Western Union and emerging digital alternatives like Wise and PayPal. The company's reliance on agent locations means service quality and availability depend on partner reliability and consistency. While MoneyGram advertises ease of use, the actual costs associated with transfers are not disclosed on this location-finder page, leaving consumers to discover fees only during the transaction process. The service model also limits MoneyGram to customers with access to physical locations, potentially excluding those in rural areas or without transportation.
pros: Extensive network of 29+ locations across Cleveland serving diverse neighborhoods and demographics
Partnership with major retailers (Walmart, CVS) offering extended operating hours until 8 PM or later
Hybrid transaction model allowing customers to initiate transfers online and complete pickup in-person
No bank account required to send or receive money
Established brand with global infrastructure for international transfers
Multiple agent types (big box retailers, check-cashing specialists, convenience stores) provide redundancy
Same-location sending and receiving capability at many locations
cons: Transfer fees and exchange rates not disclosed on website—consumers only discover costs at point of transaction
Service quality and hours dependent on individual agent partner compliance and reliability
Limited to customers near physical locations; no solution for remote rural areas without agents
Faces intense competition from cheaper digital alternatives (Wise, PayPal, digital wallets) for both domestic and international transfers
Slower than real-time digital payment options for consumers seeking immediate fund availability
best_for: Unbanked or underbanked consumers without checking accounts who need reliable money transfer services
Individuals sending remittances internationally from the U.S. to family members abroad
People in Cleveland needing quick access to funds from nearby retail locations during standard retail hours
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for unbanked consumers and people sending international remittances who value physical location accessibility and the security of established retail partnerships. The primary caveat is that pricing and competitive positioning against digital alternatives are not transparent on their website—consumers must initiate transactions to discover actual costs, and cheaper, faster digital options may now offer better value for many use cases.
services: Domestic money transfers within the United States
In-person transaction processing at agent locations
Online account access for managing transfers
Same-day transfer options at participating locations
